perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Distributor' to 'Reseller' creates confusion about the entity's role. The agreement refers to the entity as the 'Distributor' throughout the text, but this change redefines the collectively named companies as the 'Reseller'. This creates an in-text contradiction because obligations of the 'Distributor' elsewhere in the contract now become unclear in scope.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (B) Distributor purchases Products from Zebra under the Distributor Agreement;
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines the collectively named entities from 'Distributor' to 'Reseller'. This creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text, which explicitly states '(B) Distributor purchases Products from Zebra under the Distributor Agreement'. If the entity is now defined as 'Reseller', the Distributor Agreement and the purchasing obligation become unclear and contradictory. This directly contradicts the original contractual intent and introduces ambiguity about which entity is responsible for purchasing under the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: To order the Xplore Products, Distributor shall place a purchase order via sending an email to xpldistributors@zebra.com. Each purchase order shall specify the bill-to address, ship-to address, quantity and description of each Xplore Product ordered, the unit price for each Xplore Product, the requested ship date, the preferred means of delivery, and tax-exempt certifications, if any. Orders received without this information or which contain any discrepancy may be returned to Distributor for completion or revision as applicable.
perturbation.changed_text: To order the Xplore Products, Distributor shall place a purchase order via sending an email to xpldistributors@zebra.com. Each purchase order shall specify the bill-to address, ship-to address, quantity and description of each Xplore Product ordered, the unit price for each Xplore Product, the requested ship date, the preferred means of delivery, and tax-exempt certifications, if any. Orders received without this information or which contain any discrepancy may be returned to Reseller for completion or revision as applicable.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Distributor' with 'Reseller' in this section creates a contradiction because, elsewhere in the agreement, 'Distributor' is used for specific actions. If the Reseller and Distributor aren't explicitly the same, it's unclear which entity bears the responsibility for correcting the purchase orders. This undermines the clarity of the purchasing process.
perturbation.location: EXHIBIT B, ARTICLE I, 1. Issuance and Acceptance of Purchase Order
perturbation.contradicted_location: EXHIBIT B, ARTICLE I, 2. Purchase Order Rescheduling, Cancellation and Modification.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Distributor has the right to cancel, reschedule or modify all or any portion of a purchase order that has been accepted by Xplore at no cost to Distributor only if such cancellation or modification request is made within [*****] business days of PO acceptance or rescheduling request is made at least [*****] business days prior to the most current Estimated Shipping Date for that purchase order. Distributor has the right to change the destination of all or any portion of a purchase order that has been accepted by Xplore at no cost to Distributor only if such change in destination request is made at least [*****] business days prior to the most current Estimated Shipping Date for that purchase order. Except as set forth above, purchase orders are non-changeable and non- cancellable by Distributor, once accepted by Xplore. Certain purchase orders, determined at Xplore's sole discretion, and generally of large volume and/or extended lead times, may be subject to alternative rescheduling, cancellation, and modification rights. Should such purchase orders be subject to alternative rights, Xplore shall inform Distributor of alternative rights prior to order acceptance. Distributor will then have [*****] business days to accept, or reject, the alternative terms of Xplore for that certain purchase order. If Distributor rejects such alternative terms, the purchase order will not be accepted by Xplore.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Distributor' with 'Reseller' in the purchase order instruction section. The contradicted text specifies that the 'Distributor' has the right to cancel, reschedule, or modify purchase orders. By changing the initial instruction to involve 'Reseller' instead of 'Distributor', the contract creates a contradiction regarding who has the right to manage the purchase orders. This misalignment in terminology leads to a direct conflict as the contract now assigns different responsibilities to different entities, creating uncertainty and potential disputes, adhering to the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Distributor shall use commercially reasonable efforts to maintain thirty (30) days of stock in Distributor's inventory to support sales. Xplore acknowledges that from time to time, Distributor's inventory levels may fall below the thirty (30) days goal that is agreed upon by both Parties. If inventory levels fall below the thirty (30) day goal for more than sixty (60) consecutive days, Xplore, upon written notice to Distributor, shall replenish the stock to an amount agreed by both Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: Distributor shall use commercially reasonable efforts to maintain thirty (30) days of stock in Distributor's inventory to support sales. Xplore acknowledges that from time to time, Reseller's inventory levels may fall below the thirty (30) days goal that is agreed upon by both Parties. If inventory levels fall below the thirty (30) day goal for more than sixty (60) consecutive days, Xplore, upon written notice to Distributor, shall replenish the stock to an amount agreed by both Parties.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Distributor' to 'Reseller' in the second sentence of this section creates confusion. The section begins by stating the 'Distributor' should hold stock, then mentions 'Reseller's' inventory. Since the term 'Reseller' was used to collectively define the group in the first few paragraphs, this causes an in-text contradiction as 'Distributor' is being refferred as one company whereas, 'Reseller' is being reffered as a group of different companies, contradicting the purpose.
perturbation.location: EXHIBIT B, ARTICLE I, 4. Stock on Hand.
perturbation.contradicted_location: EXHIBIT B, ARTICLE I, 5. Product Return and Stock Rotation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The terms of Section 3 of Schedule 2 of the Distribution Agreement will apply to Xplore Products, provided however that stock rotation allowance for Xplore Products will be based on the net dollar value of Distributor's purchases in each calendar quarter of Xplore Products and such allowance will be calculated separate and apart from all other Products purchased by Distributor during such period.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Distributor' to 'Reseller' introduces ambiguity regarding whose purchases are relevant for stock rotation allowance. The original text refers to 'Distributor' in both the changed and contradicted sections, maintaining consistency. By changing 'Distributor' to 'Reseller' in the changed text, it creates an inconsistency. The contradicted text states that stock rotation allowance will be based on the net dollar value of 'Distributor's' purchases. The changed text creates a contradiction because it refers to 'Reseller's' inventory, while the contradicted text refers to 'Distributor's' purchases, thus triggering a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction, as the term Reseller was used to collectively define the group in the first few paragraphs, this causes an in-text contradiction as 'Distributor' is being refferred as one company whereas, 'Reseller' is being reffered as a group of different companies, contradicting the purpose, impacting the calculation of stock rotation allowance.
